metadata.name: Japanese Paragraphs
metadata.description: Learner-friendly multi-sentence Japanese reading passages with sentence-level chunking.

schema.label: ID
schema.description: Stable unique identifier for the passage.

schema.label: Title
schema.description: Short learner-friendly title for the passage.

schema.label: Japanese
schema.description: Full paragraph or short passage in natural Japanese surface form.

schema.label: English
schema.description: Natural English translation of the full passage.

schema.label: Notes
schema.description: Passage-level notes about tone, context, or reading strategy.

schema.label: Tags
schema.description: Optional machine-friendly tags for filtering or grouping.

schema.label: Sentences
schema.description: Ordered sentences composing the passage. Each sentence may have its own chunk list.

paragraphSentence.description: A sentence inside a longer learner reading passage.

fields.label: Sentence Japanese
fields.description: Japanese sentence exactly as it appears in the passage.

fields.label: Sentence English
fields.description: Natural English translation of the standalone sentence.

fields.label: Pattern
fields.description: Reusable grammar pattern highlighted in the sentence.

fields.label: Chunks
fields.description: Ordered learner chunks inside the sentence.

sentenceChunk.description: A learner reading unit inside a sentence within a paragraph.

fields.label: Chunk Japanese
fields.description: Japanese surface text for this chunk exactly as it appears.

fields.label: Grammar Focus
fields.description: Canonical grammar or reading focus for this chunk.

fields.label: References
fields.description: Lookup keys for content words or grammar patterns used in this chunk.

entries.title: 駅へ急ぐ朝
entries.ja: 山本さんは毎日渋谷で会社まで歩いて行きます。でも今日は遅くなったので、六時十五分の電車に乗ろうと思いました。階段を下りているとき、くつのひもがほどけているのに気づきましたが、電車も来るのが見えました。『走れ！』と思って、転ばないようにくつをぬいで、電車に乗ることができました。
entries.en: Mr. Yamamoto walks to his company in Shibuya every day. But today he ended up late, so he decided to try to catch the 6:15 train. While going down the stairs, he noticed his shoelace was untied, but he could also see the train arriving. He thought, 'Run!', took off his shoe so he would not trip, and managed to get on the train.
notes: Built as a short reading passage from familiar daily-life vocabulary and movement verbs.
The story is slightly playful, but the grammar stays learner-friendly.

sentences.ja: 山本さんは毎日渋谷で会社まで歩いて行きます。
sentences.en: Mr. Yamamoto walks to his company in Shibuya every day.

chunks.ja: 山本さんは毎日渋谷で
chunks.gloss: As for Mr. Yamamoto, every day in Shibuya

chunks.ja: 会社まで歩いて行きます
chunks.gloss: walks and goes to the company

sentences.ja: でも今日は遅くなったので、六時十五分の電車に乗ろうと思いました。
sentences.en: But today he ended up late, so he thought he would take the 6:15 train.

chunks.ja: でも今日は遅くなったので、
chunks.gloss: but because today he became late,
he thought he would ride it

sentences.ja: 階段を下りているとき、くつのひもがほどけているのに気づきましたが、電車も来るのが見えました。
sentences.en: While going down the stairs, he noticed his shoelace was untied, but he could also see the train arriving.
sentences.pattern: 〜ているとき / 〜のに気づく / 〜のが見える

chunks.ja: 階段を下りているとき、
くつのひもがほどけているのに気づきましたが、
chunks.gloss: he noticed that his shoelace was untied, but

chunks.ja: 電車も来るのが見えました
chunks.gloss: he could also see the train coming

sentences.ja: 『走れ！』と思って、転ばないようにくつをぬいで、電車に乗ることができました。
sentences.en: He thought, 'Run!', took off his shoe so he would not trip, and managed to get on the train.
sentences.pattern: 命令形 / 〜ように / 〜ことができる

chunks.ja: 転ばないようにくつをぬいで、
chunks.gloss: taking off his shoe so he would not trip,

chunks.ja: 電車に乗ることができました
chunks.gloss: he was able to get on the train

entries.title: 雨の日の図書館
entries.ja: 午後に雨が強くなったので、私は図書館に入りました。窓の近くの静かな席で本を読んでいると、外の音が少し遠く感じられました。時間がゆっくり過ぎて、帰るころには気分も落ち着いていました。
entries.en: When the rain got stronger in the afternoon, I went into the library. As I read a book in a quiet seat near the window, the sounds outside felt a little far away. Time passed slowly, and by the time I went home, I felt calm again.
notes: This passage leans into reading-comprehension mood words rather than dialogue.

sentences.ja: 午後に雨が強くなったので、私は図書館に入りました。
sentences.en: Because the rain got stronger in the afternoon, I went into the library.

chunks.ja: 午後に雨が強くなったので、
chunks.gloss: because the rain became stronger in the afternoon,

chunks.ja: 私は図書館に入りました

sentences.ja: 窓の近くの静かな席で本を読んでいると、外の音が少し遠く感じられました。
sentences.en: As I was reading a book in a quiet seat near the window, the sounds outside felt a little distant.

chunks.ja: 窓の近くの静かな席で
chunks.gloss: in a quiet seat near the window

chunks.ja: 外の音が少し遠く感じられました
chunks.gloss: the outside sounds felt a little distant

sentences.ja: 時間がゆっくり過ぎて、帰るころには気分も落ち着いていました。
sentences.en: Time passed slowly, and by the time I went home, my mood had calmed down too.

chunks.ja: 時間がゆっくり過ぎて、

chunks.gloss: by the time I went home

chunks.ja: 気分も落ち着いていました
chunks.gloss: my mood had calmed down too

entries.title: THE HERO!! ～怒れる拳に火をつけろ～
entries.ja:
ONE PUNCH！
(Three！ Two！ One！ Kill shot！)
参上！　必勝！　至上最強！！
なんだってんだ？　フラストレーション　俺は止まらない

ONE PUNCH！　完了！　連戦連勝！
俺は喝！！　常に勝つ！！　圧勝！！

Power！ Get power！　ギリギリ限界まで

HERO　俺を讃える声や　喝采なんて　欲しくはないさ
HERO　だから　人知れず　悪と闘う
(Nobody knows who he is.)
天(そら)覆い　押し寄せる敵　俺は背を向けはしない
HERO　ならば　揺るぎなき覚悟したため　繰り出せ鉄拳

(Three！ Two！ One！ Fight back！)
参上！ Go on！　正々堂々！！
どうなってんだ？　なにも感じねぇ　もはや敵居ねー！

JUSTICE！　執行！　問答無用！
俺が断つ！！　悪を断つ！！　合掌！！

Power！ Get power！　アドレナリン溢れ出すぜ！
Power！ Get power！　鍛えた技をぶちかませ！

HERO　どんなに　強い奴も　ちっぽけなガキだったんだ
HERO　弱き　己乗り越え　強くなる
(Nobody knows who he is.)
神宿る　拳掲げて　俺は突き進むだけさ
HERO　いつか　敗北に汚泥なめるまで　闘うHERO

俺は諦めない　その胸に未来(あす)を描き
目覚め行く　世界へ今　舞い上がれ　強く高く
どんな時でも　なにがあっても

HERO　俺を讃える声や　喝采なんて　欲しくはないさ
HERO　だから　人知れず　悪と闘う
(Nobody knows who he is.)
神宿る　拳掲げて　俺は突き進むだけさ
HERO　いつか　敗北に汚泥なめるまで　闘うHERO
孤独なHERO
entries.en:
ONE PUNCH!
(Three! Two! One! Kill shot!)
Here I come! Certain victory! The absolute strongest!!
What is this? Frustration—I will not stop.

ONE PUNCH! Complete! Battle after battle, victory after victory!
I roar!! I always win!! Total victory!!

Power! Get power! Right up to the very limit.

HERO—I do not want voices praising me or applause.
HERO—so I fight evil without anyone knowing.
(Nobody knows who he is.)
As enemies surge in and cover the sky, I will not turn my back.
HERO—then, with unwavering resolve prepared, unleash the iron fist.

(Three! Two! One! Fight back!)
Here I come! Go on! Fair and square!!
What is going on? I do not feel anything anymore—there are no enemies left!

JUSTICE! Execute! No questions asked!
I cut down evil!! I sever evil!! Hands together in prayer!!

Power! Get power! Adrenaline is overflowing!
Power! Get power! Unleash the techniques you have trained!

HERO—even the strongest guy was once just a tiny kid.
HERO—overcoming the weak self, one becomes strong.
(Nobody knows who he is.)
With a god dwelling in my raised fist, I simply keep charging ahead.
HERO—until someday I taste defeat in the mud, I will fight, HERO.

I will not give up; I draw the future in my heart.
Into the awakening world now—rise up, strong and high.
At any time, no matter what happens.

HERO—I do not want voices praising me or applause.
HERO—so I fight evil without anyone knowing.
(Nobody knows who he is.)
With a god dwelling in my raised fist, I simply keep charging ahead.
HERO—until someday I taste defeat in the mud, I will fight, HERO.
A lonely HERO.
